title: Evangelion 111 Aired on Toonami March 16
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 1.11 Aired on Toonami March 16             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Mar 10, 2013 2:28 pm Yep.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Mar 10, 2013 2:58 pm If it's not in Japanese with subtitles, I'll have to rip some faces. But yay, Evangelion!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Mar 10, 2013 3:40 pm ANN link: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2013-03-10/evangelion-1.11-to-run-on-adult-swim-toonami-block Don't see the point in watching something I already own and so late at night, but this is pretty neat.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Mar 10, 2013 3:41 pm That's awesome. I wonder if they'll follow suit with 2.22 and 3.33 (when it gets released here).

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Sun Mar 10, 2013 4:24 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: If it's not in Japanese with subtitles, I'll have to rip some faces. But yay, Evangelion!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Mar 10, 2013 4:36 pm Hey, at least your Voice Actors are decent, have ever heard how dubbed animes in general sounds in French?!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Mar 10, 2013 4:38 pm This is a Good Thing. Count your blessings 'murca, us Brits get the occasional Ghibli film or Akira on Channel Four and thats about it.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Mar 10, 2013 4:39 pm Yes! I've always wanted to watch a movie I own on Bluray with terrible Texas voice "actors" & commercial breaks every 5 minutes!

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Mar 10, 2013 4:44 pm Well if one person watches the dub, buys the DVD and discovers the original version, then this is still a Good Thing.

[]:             Quote Post by TheStentch » Sun Mar 10, 2013 5:06 pm I thought it was the 17th as per the tweet?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Mar 10, 2013 5:51 pm This is pretty cool news. It's been what 7-8 years since any evangelion content's been aired on Cartoon Network? It might end up bolstering the fandom in a similar manner to when the series was aired on Adult Swim.....though probably not as significantly as this is a one-off movie airing. I wonder how severe it'll be edited?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Mar 10, 2013 6:02 pm Guy Nacks wrote: This is pretty cool news. It's been what 7-8 years since any evangelion content's been aired on Cartoon Network? It might end up bolstering the fandom in a similar manner to when the series was aired on Adult Swim.....though probably not as significantly as this is a one-off movie airing.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Mar 10, 2013 6:14 pm Not sure if I'll watch it (want to keep my virgin ears) but this is pretty freaking cool. I'm curious how much of this is Funi's doing and how much is CN's, though. Is Funi trying to capitalize on the upcoming 3.0 hype and licensing it to AS on the cheap or is CN upping their investment in Toonami? Probably a little bit of both I suppose.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Mar 10, 2013 6:32 pm Trailer: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xO3QQTrSchs

[]:             Quote Post by lastcody » Sun Mar 10, 2013 6:50 pm This is rad but whats going to come out of it is SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Mar 10, 2013 7:07 pm Ornette wrote: Trailer: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xO3QQTrSchs

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Mar 10, 2013 7:48 pm "The Rebuild movies are a sequel to the original series, though how﻿ they are related to eachother hasn't been revealed."

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Mar 10, 2013 8:27 pm Jayfive wrote: Oh youtube comments. You never fail me

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Sun Mar 10, 2013 8:32 pm CJD wrote: Not like the sequel theory isn't relatively popular to start with.

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Sun Mar 10, 2013 8:49 pm lastcody wrote: SPOILER: Show
